rationale: Neither candidate surfaces dsocek's actual substantive concern (fused-projection naming conflicts, driving the mapper from _packed_modules_mapping), so recall is low for both, though X's push toward a single source of truth for component discovery is thematically closer. X offers two concrete, file/line-anchored, non-blocking suggestions (consolidate hardcoded component lists via _dit_modules; warn on zero-layer LoRA application) that are actionable and plausible even if unverifiable in isolation. Y is essentially a validation sweep confirming the diff does what it claims, safer and well-grounded in the diff but adds little beyond restating correctness, ending in a bare APPROVE with no concrete asks. X edges out on usefulness and actionability despite slightly higher fabrication risk.
